records.ety: [3d keel + haul: cf. LG. & D. kielhalen, G. kielholen. ]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Keelhauled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Keelhauling.]

defs: To haul under the keel of a ship, by ropes attached to the yardarms on each side. It was formerly practiced as a punishment in the Dutch and English navies.
